qa_pairs.question: How do users request a single A100-40gb GPU on the Narval cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: To request a single A100-40gb GPU on the Narval cluster, users should use the Slurm option `--gpus=a100:1`.

qa_pairs.question: What types of Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) instances are available on Narval, and how can they be requested for a computing task?
qa_pairs.answer: Narval offers four sizes of Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) instances: 1g.5gb (1/8 of the compute power with 5 GB of GPU memory), 2g.10gb (2/8 of the compute power with 10 GB of GPU memory), 3g.20gb (3/8 of the compute power with 20 GB of GPU memory), and 4g.20gb (4/8 of the compute power with 20 GB of GPU memory). To request a single instance for a computing task, the corresponding Slurm options are: `--gpus=a100_1g.5gb:1`, `--gpus=a100_2g.10gb:1`, `--gpus=a100_3g.20gb:1`, or `--gpus=a100_4g.20gb:1`.

qa_pairs.question: How can users monitor their CPU and GPU computing tasks on the Narval cluster?
qa_pairs.answer: Users can monitor their CPU and GPU computing tasks in real-time or view past tasks through the Narval portal at `https://portail.narval.calculquebec.ca/`. This portal allows users to visualize resource usage, including compute cores, memory, and GPUs, to optimize resource utilization and reduce queue times.
